stories.title: The Wyvern-Priest's Wager
stories.text:
In the Hearthlands city of Korinth, the high temple of Vesh the Storm-Bringer stood ringed by war-drums and torch-smoke. Priest-Commander Aldis had wagered his authority on a prophecy: that a wyvern would descend from the Ironspine peaks to seal a dying crown's succession. The Crown's regent laughed—until scales darkened the temple roof. The wyvern's name was Keth-Vex, collector of debts older than kingdoms. She had not come to bless anyone. The priest knelt as the wyvern landed, her copper eyes fixed on his throat. "The miracle happened," Keth-Vex hissed. "Now your price walks through that door."

It was Aldis's daughter—a scholar, childless, unknown to the Crown. The wyvern demanded she serve as living hostage in the luminous vaults beneath the world, tending Keth-Vex's memory-hoard for seven years. Aldis's hand moved to his ceremonial blade. Then he looked at the assembled priests, at the Crown's wavering courtiers, at the miracle still burning in the temple stones. He lowered his hands. "Seven years," he whispered. His daughter stepped forward, unafraid, and climbed onto the wyvern's back. As they rose into the smoke-drunk sky, Aldis understood: the miracle had two prices. One paid in blood-oath. The other in love.

stories.title: The Refugee's Ledger
stories.text: At Pale Crossing waystation, scribe Merrin discovers that the refugee rolls no longer reconcile—names appear twice, or not at all, and the phantom-whisper echoes from the margins of parchment. When Caravan Master Dorn brings word that villages beyond the Scarred Expanse are still vanishing, Merrin realizes the ledgers aren't errors: they're a map of what's being erased. To stop the erasure, Merrin must choose between burning the records (and losing proof those people ever existed) or delivering them to the shadow-brokers who promise to preserve the names—for a price measured in memory. By story's end, the ledgers are distributed across Vex's shadow markets, and Merrin has forgotten the face of everyone they tried to save.

stories.title: The Song of the Deep
stories.text: Captain Meren Blackwater, still indebted to the sea itself after securing the Tear of the Drowned God, is conscripted by Commander Vesta to investigate the intensifying harmonic resonance at the Sunken Cathedrals. What Meren discovers in the trench-depths is worse than anticipated: the transformed merchants, the pre-human dead, and something older—a civilization singing itself back into existence from beneath the world's skin. Meren's curse allows them to hear the song's true meaning: it's a summons. The transformed are being called home to rebuild what the Luminous Ascension erased. Meren must decide whether to report this to Vesta or help the transformed complete their pilgrimage, knowing either choice will collect the sea's debt in blood.

stories.title: The Phantom's Heir
stories.text: A young scholar born in the shadow of the Luminous Ascension begins speaking in languages that predate human kingdoms—the same tongues carved into the Singing Catacombs and sung by the pre-human dead. They are not possessed; they are becoming a vessel for a phantom-echo fragment that seeks reassembly through deep knowledge rather than theft. Ashara the Archer, still haunted by the Memory-Thief's pursuit, takes the scholar under protection. As their bond deepens, Ashara discovers that proximity to the scholar only accelerates the Echo's integration.

stories.title: The Shadow Market's Price
stories.text: Vex the Shade-Merchant's shadow-markets have become the Dominion's black trade in world-fragments and stolen memories, drawing desperate refugees seeking fragments of their own lost pasts. When Vex discovers that the Archivist is assembling these fragments not to restore alternate timelines but to open a doorway into the void—to reach something that existed before reality itself was rewritten—Vex faces a crisis of commerce: continue brokering dangerous goods and profit from the world's fracturing, or expose the Archivist and lose the only currency that still holds value in a collapsing Dominion. Vex chooses a third path: creating counterfeit fragments and flooding the black market with false memories, destabilizing the Archivist's grand working. The cost is that Vex's own identity becomes uncertain—every shadow Vex now inhabits carries a false memory.

stories.title: The Bone-Singer's Harmony
stories.text: Kess of the Bone-Singer becomes aware that the pre-human dead they've been controlling are no longer obeying bone-song commands—instead, they're singing in response, achieving harmonic resonance with the transformed merchants and the depths-dwellers. The skeleton-songs are no longer tools of Kess's will; they're part of a larger convergence. When Kess attempts to silence the dead, they resist, and Kess realizes the horrible truth: the dead remember what they were before death, and they're choosing to sing themselves toward resurrection. Kess must decide whether to break the bones of those they've loved (destroying the singers) or step aside and allow the dead to rebuild themselves, knowing what comes back may not be human.

stories.title: The Auditor's Secret
stories.text: Sevin the Auditor, having discovered that phantom ledgers are prophecy rather than error, is approached by a representative of the Regent-Keeper with an impossible offer: Sevin can become the new keeper of the Ledger of Names—the Crown's deepest secret—and use ledger-magic to slowly restore order to the fractured Dominion. All Sevin must do is suppress knowledge of the Luminous Ascension's true nature and convince others that reality was never rewritten. The price is compartmentalized truth: Sevin would know the world is broken but would spend their life convincing everyone it's whole. By the story's end, Sevin accepts the role, inheriting a terrible loneliness and the knowledge that every entry they make in the restored ledger is a lie meant to save civilization.

stories.title: The Deepwarden's Binding
stories.text: Sylas the Deepwarden's ritual to bind the Abyssal Rift is reaching critical mass, but the deepkin council discovers that finishing the binding will trap not only the rifted entities but also the deepkin themselves in a permanent half-state—neither free nor drowned, anchored to the ritual's magic for eternity. When Sylas learns that Captain Meren's curse is intertwined with the binding's success (Meren's debt to the sea will seal the rift but also seal Meren beneath it), Sylas must choose between completing the binding (saving the surface world but dooming Meren) or allowing the rift to remain open (keeping the deepkin free but risking surface invasion). The story ends with Sylas performing a modified binding that leaves a permanent scar—a wound that will never fully heal but that keeps both worlds partially alive.

stories.title: The Wanderer's Question
stories.text: The Faceless Wanderer—the fractured Ascension consciousness that may be the world learning to question itself—encounters a scholar seeking proof that the Luminous Ascension was either preventable or necessary. The Wanderer offers an unsettling answer: the old world was rewritten because it had already been rewritten countless times before, and each rewrite brought someone to ask this same question. The scholar realizes they're caught in a recursive loop—that asking whether the catastrophe was necessary IS the catastrophe, an endless recursion of doubt eating reality from the inside. By the story's end, the scholar must choose to stop asking (and accept the rewrite) or continue asking (and become complicit in perpetuating the loop). The Wanderer watches with something like sorrow.

stories.title: The Singing Scar
stories.text: In the aftermath of the Sunken Cathedrals' harmonic crescendo, a scar opens in reality itself—a thin place where the singing leaks through into the waking world. Those who hear the singing are drawn toward transformation, but those who resist it are torn between two worlds: partially transformed, neither merchant nor mortal, caught in a state of beautiful agony. A young warrior seeks out the scar intending to cross into whatever lies beyond and find a loved one transformed by the Luminous Ascension. What they discover is that the scar doesn't lead anywhere—the transformed exist in a state of perpetual arrival, always approaching but never reaching the other side. The warrior must decide whether to join them in the in-between or return to the mortal world and live knowing their beloved is forever just out of reach.
